function.description: Run a targeted workspace shell command; prefer `rg`/`rg --files` and pruned `find`, not `ls -R` or broad tree scans.
function.name: bash

function.description: Read a scoped slice of a known text file; prefer narrow scopes and avoid full-file reads on large files.
function.name: read_file

scope.description: Optional narrowed read scope. Use 'line:start-end' or 'char:start-end'.

function.description: Write a text file in the workspace.
function.name: write_file

function.description: Replace text in a workspace file.
function.name: edit_file

function.description: Find likely paths first; keep patterns narrow and skip dependency/build/generated directories unless they are in scope.
function.name: glob_search

function.description: Search within a narrowed path or glob; prefer targeted filters and avoid sweeping dependency/build/generated trees.
function.name: grep_search

function.description: Fetch a URL, convert it into readable text, and answer a prompt about it.
function.name: WebFetch

function.description: Search the web for current information and return cited results.
function.name: WebSearch

function.description: Update the structured task list for the current session.
function.name: TodoWrite

function.description: Compatibility wrapper: create a specialized subagent and immediately execute the task in `description`; use `prompt` for extra instructions, not as the final answer.
function.name: Agent

function.description: Search for deferred or specialized tools by exact name or keywords.
function.name: ToolSearch

function.description: Wait for a specified duration without holding a shell process.
function.name: Sleep

function.description: Send a message to the user.
function.name: SendUserMessage

function.description: Get or set Claude Code settings.
function.name: Config

function.description: Return structured output in the requested format.
function.name: StructuredOutput

function.description: Execute code in a REPL-like subprocess.
function.name: REPL

function.description: Execute a PowerShell command with optional timeout.
function.name: PowerShell

function.description: Unavailable in this environment. Prefer inspecting the workspace or making a reasonable assumption instead of using this tool.
function.name: AskUserQuestion

function.description: Create a background task that runs in a separate subprocess.
function.name: TaskCreate

function.description: Create a background task from a structured task packet.
function.name: RunTaskPacket

function.description: Get the status and details of a background task by ID.
function.name: TaskGet

function.description: List all background tasks and their current status.
function.name: TaskList

function.description: Stop a running background task by ID.
function.name: TaskStop

function.description: Send a message or update to a running background task.
function.name: TaskUpdate

function.description: Retrieve the output produced by a background task.
function.name: TaskOutput

function.description: Access the memory pool which stores all user messages and LLM outputs from the current session. Use action 'list' to retrieve recent entries (with optional limit), 'search' to find entries matching a query string, or 'count' to get the total number of entries.
function.name: memory_pool

action.description: The action to perform: 'list' (recent entries), 'search' (find by query), or 'count' (total entries).

query.description: Search query string. Required when action is 'search'.

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return for 'list' action. Defaults to 50.

function.name: scratchpad_write
function.description: Write a value to the run-scoped in-RAM scratchpad under a named key. The scratchpad is cleared at the start of each run and never written to disk. Use it to pass large outputs between agents by reference instead of inlining them in messages.

function.name: scratchpad_read
function.description: Read a value from the run-scoped in-RAM scratchpad by key.

function.name: scratchpad_list
function.description: List all keys currently stored in the run-scoped in-RAM scratchpad, with their sizes.

function.name: scratchpad_delete
function.description: Delete a key from the run-scoped in-RAM scratchpad to free memory.
Create a subagent; it stays idle until you pass `initial_message` here or call `send_message` with its `agent_id`.
function.name: spawn_agent

role.description: Short label for this agent's responsibility (e.g. 'coder', 'critic', 'planner').

system_prompt.description: Full system-level instruction that defines the agent's persona, constraints, and objectives.

model.description: Local LM Studio model key to use for this agent. It must already exist on this computer; guessed or remote-only model names are rejected. Defaults to the caller's own model if omitted.

context_limit.description: Maximum token budget for this agent's context window. Omit to use the model default.

tools.description: List of tool names the agent is allowed to call. Omit to give the worker the default non-manager tool set.

initial_message.description: Optional first user message for the newly spawned agent.

await_reply.description: Whether to execute that first message immediately and wait for the reply. Defaults to true.

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs to attach to the agent for routing or bookkeeping.

function.description: Send a user-role message to a specific agent and optionally wait for its response. Managers can delegate to any agent; non-managers may only message themselves.
function.name: send_message

agent_id.description: ID of the target agent returned by spawn_agent.

content.description: Text content of the message to inject as a user turn.

attachments.description: Optional file paths or data blobs to include alongside the message.

await_reply.description: If true, block until the agent produces a response and return it inline. If false, return immediately and poll with get_agent_state. Defaults to true.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum milliseconds to wait when await_reply is true before returning a timeout error.

function.description: Spawn multiple agents and run them all in parallel, waiting for every one to finish before returning. Each entry in the 'agents' array defines a worker (role, system_prompt, message). Requires parallel inference to be enabled in Global Policy. The number of agents must not exceed the configured max_parallel_agents limit. The manager is blocked until all workers complete.
function.name: send_parallel

agents.description: List of agents to spawn and run concurrently. Each entry must include role, system_prompt, and message.

role.description: Short label for the agent's responsibility (e.g. 'analyzer', 'coder').

system_prompt.description: Full system instruction defining the agent's persona and constraints.

initial_message.description: Task message to send to the agent as its first user turn.

model.description: Local LM Studio model key. Defaults to the caller's model if omitted.

function.description: Read the conversation history of a specific agent, including estimated remaining context budget. Managers may inspect any agent; non-managers may inspect only themselves.
function.name: read_agent_messages

agent_id.description: ID of the agent whose messages to read.

roles.description: Filter by message role(s). Options: 'user', 'assistant', 'tool'. Omit to return all roles.

limit.description: Return only the most recent N messages. Omit to return the full history.

offset.description: Skip the first N messages (for pagination).

function.description: List all active and recently terminated agents managed by this session, with their current status, role, model, and token usage. Use this to get a global view of the multi-agent system before deciding how to route work.
function.name: list_agents

status_filter.description: Only return agents in these states. Options: 'running', 'idle', 'paused', 'done', 'error'. Omit for all.

role_filter.description: Only return agents whose role matches this string.

function.description: Retrieve the detailed runtime state of a single agent, including estimated used and remaining context budget.
function.name: get_agent_state

agent_id.description: ID of the agent to inspect.

function.description: Terminate a running agent immediately and release its resources. The agent's conversation history is preserved for read_agent_messages until the session ends.
function.name: kill_agent

agent_id.description: ID of the agent to terminate.

reason.description: Human-readable reason for termination, stored in the agent's final state.

function.description: Pause the inference loop of an agent after its current generation step completes. The agent will not process further messages until resume_agent is called. Useful for checkpointing or waiting for external conditions.
function.name: pause_agent

agent_id.description: ID of the agent to pause.

function.description: Resume the inference loop of a previously paused agent. Any messages sent while the agent was paused are delivered in order.
function.name: resume_agent

agent_id.description: ID of the agent to resume.

function.description: Send the same message to multiple agents simultaneously. By default this waits for concrete replies from each agent; set `await_reply` to false only when you explicitly want queue-only delivery.
function.name: broadcast_message

agent_ids.description: List of agent IDs to receive the message. Pass ['*'] to target every active agent.

content.description: Text content of the message to send to all targets.

await_reply.description: If true, wait for all agents to respond before returning. Defaults to true.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum milliseconds to wait per agent when await_reply is true.

function.description: Fork an existing agent by duplicating its conversation history and system prompt into a new agent. The fork starts from the same context state but runs independently. Useful for exploring divergent reasoning paths or A/B testing prompting strategies.
function.name: fork_agent

source_agent_id.description: ID of the agent to fork from.

role.description: Role label for the new forked agent. Defaults to the source agent's role with a '-fork' suffix.

system_prompt_override.description: If provided, replace the forked agent's system prompt instead of inheriting the source agent's.

truncate_at.description: Fork from this message index in the conversation history, discarding later messages. Omit to fork from the latest state.

function.description: Collect the last assistant output from a set of agents and merge them into a single structured result. Optionally ask a synthesis prompt to a designated aggregator model that combines the outputs. Use this at the end of a parallel fan-out to consolidate findings.
function.name: aggregate_results

agent_ids.description: Agents whose last output to collect. Pass ['*'] for all active agents.

synthesis_prompt.description: Optional prompt sent to a synthesis model to merge the collected outputs into a unified answer. If omitted, the raw per-agent outputs are returned as-is.

synthesis_model.description: Model to use for synthesis. Defaults to the manager's own model.

format.description: Output format for the aggregated result: 'raw' (list of outputs), 'summary' (prose), or 'structured' (JSON object keyed by agent_id).

function.name: pipe_message
function.description: Forward a specific message to an agent. Use this to chain agents: after receiving a response from agent A via send_message, explicitly pass that content (or any excerpt of it) to agent B. The manager selects exactly what to forward.

content.description: The exact content to send to the target agent. Typically the response you received from a previous send_message call.

to_agent_id.description: ID of the agent that will receive the content.

prefix.description: Optional instruction to prepend before the content (e.g. 'Review this code:').

suffix.description: Optional instruction to append after the content (e.g. 'Be concise.').
